birthEvaluation.generatorId: materials_descriptor_ablation_generator with family-grouped holdout and source-family matched controls

gates.message: HardSeed birth requires a real external problem anchor before generator evidence can count.
External problem anchor must cite a public-safe external source.
External problem anchor must define a measured target outcome.
External problem anchor must define the known baseline or prior rival state.
External problem anchor must explain domain scientific significance beyond pipeline, package, or tool capability.
External problem anchor must define a discovery-scored outcome that is not only operational pipeline success.
External problem anchor must bind public-safe significance evidence refs into the evidence set.
External problem anchor must be inspectable through the evidence refs.
Generator-only signals are blocked before HardSeed birth.
Package-only or pipeline-contract-only signals are blocked before HardSeed birth.
Internally interesting but externally unanchored signals are blocked before HardSeed birth.
Source-family documented behavior is blocked before HardSeed birth.
Known or trivial behavior is blocked before HardSeed birth.
HardSeed birth requires an external problem anchor with public inspectability and non-internal value before runtime evidence can count.
HardSeed birth requires loaded, executed, generated, or checked runtime evidence.
Deterministic design profiles are planning evidence only; HardSeed birth requires real loaded/executed/generated target evidence.
HardSeed birth requires public-safe source and evidence refs, including a concrete public URL.
HardSeed birth is blocked when any simple baseline explains the signal.
HardSeed birth is blocked when residual magnitude is below the second-stage pressure floor of 0.1.
HardSeed birth requires at least one rival mechanism to be weakened or scoped.
HardSeed birth requires a nontrivial residual beyond metadata, tool, pipeline, or baseline success.
HardSeed birth requires cross-source or cross-slice recurrence before InsightCandidate birth is possible.
HardSeed birth is blocked when counterexamples collapse the claim.
HardSeed birth requires an independent holdout/replay path or bounded nonfatal caveat.

hardSeed.claim: Discovery-grade external anchor DISC-ANCHOR-MATBENCH-DIELECTRIC-GAP produced a birth-eligible HardSeed for holdout experimental band-gap residual after composition-only, size, and source-family baselines. This is not an InsightCandidate or Fund; it only authorizes downstream mechanism-first pressure from runtime evidence that survived birth gates.
hardSeed.observation: Anchor runtime check DISC-ANCHOR-MATBENCH-DIELECTRIC-GAP-runtime-check-02 measured outcome 1.1487 with residual 0.6376 and survived baseline, rival, counterexample, recurrence, holdout, and replay birth-gate pressure.

externalProblemAnchor.problemStatement: Matbench experimental band-gap targets expose public materials-property outcomes where descriptor ablation can test whether chemistry and formula-family mechanisms predict residuals beyond composition-only baselines.
externalProblemAnchor.measuredTargetOutcome: holdout experimental band-gap residual after composition-only, size, and source-family baselines
externalProblemAnchor.knownBaselineOrPrior: composition-only descriptors explain the residual; training-set density and material family explain the residual; measurement provenance or missingness explains the residual
externalProblemAnchor.externalValueRationale: Discovery-grade anchor selected because it is public, inspectable, outcome-bearing, and not merely generator/pipeline-internal.
externalProblemAnchor.domainScientificSignificance: A stable residual mechanism for experimental band-gap prediction would improve inspectable materials-property modeling and could expose where chemistry descriptors add scientific value beyond composition labels.
externalProblemAnchor.discoveryScoredOutcome: A discovery-scored outcome would be an externally inspectable mechanism claim about band-gap residuals that survives family holdout, descriptor ablation, rival baselines, and replay.
